summary: Excellent matrix recording with strong low end and dynamic clarity; the show delivers a memorable Dew transition and an energetic first set.
review: The tape is widely praised as the best‑sounding source from this night – a matrix/AUD blend that offers superior dynamics, pronounced bass and a pleasant reverb sheen.  The performance itself is short but solid, with an especially nice segue into “Dew” and a lively first set that many fans remember fondly.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 4‑5 stars) and repeated praise for both the audio fidelity and the musical performance lead to a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Dynamics superior to Oade/Beyer tapes, prominent but pleasant reverb, deep bottom‑end response; slightly less clean than some board recordings but overall crisp and balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Short but well‑structured set; first set described as awesome, second set ends on an interesting high note with a notable Dew transition.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Moderate whensing and head‑shaking in his vocal/sliding style were noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Gets a little wacky during Good Lovin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Positive nod to Brent Mydland’s songwriting ("I don't need you...").
